message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should quote 33 G for a single rune (25 base + 2.5 first-insurance + 5 fee = 32.5 -> 33)"
Expected: single rune (or moonstone) component → premium 33
Current error: NaN (BASE_PREMIUMS["rune"] is undefined)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Add component handling: components are items with type "rune" or "moonstone" — base premium is 25 G each. Components don't have material/enchantment/cursed (so don't apply those rules). First-insurance applies per item just like other items. Use the simplest approach (treat "rune"/"moonstone" with base 25). The block-of-3 rule will be added in a later test.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.

input.old_string: type Item = { type: string; material: string; enchantment: number; cursed: boolean };
input.new_string: type Item = { type: string; material?: string; enchantment?: number; cursed?: boolean };

content.text:
All 6 tests passing.

🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added `rune: 25` and `moonstone: 25` to `BASE_PREMIUMS`, and made `material`, `enchantment`, `cursed` optional on the `Item` type so component items (which lack those fields) typecheck.
**Result**: All tests now pass (6 passing, 33 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal — components naturally flow through existing `itemPremium` logic (base + 10% first-insurance) because they have no material/enchantment/cursed rules yet, and `Math.ceil` handles the 32.5 → 33 rounding. No block-of-3 logic added (deferred to its own test).
